Preserve original WAV format on save

Save and export now write back in the original format (PCM 16/24-bit
or IEEE float 32-bit, mono or stereo) instead of always converting to
stereo 16-bit PCM.

repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Fix recording race condition and improve recorded file handling

- Add "stopping" state to poll sampler finalization before loading WAV,
  preventing "No waveform data" error from async stop
- Show "SAVING..." screen while waiting for WAV to finalize
- Track recorded files as unsaved (dirty) with discard-on-exit cleanup
- Add timestamp to edit filenames (sample_edit_0306_1423.wav)
- Clean up recording UI header (remove debug VU display)
- Add dirty param support to DSP plugin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Remap knobs: E4=vertical scale, E5=gain/threshold, E7/E8=twist actions

- E3: zoom only (removed shift+E3 vertical scale)
- E4: vertical scale for all modes (was shift+E3)
- E5: gain/normalize in trim/loop, threshold in slice auto (was E4)
- E7 touch+twist: toggle loop on/off
- E8 touch+twist: snap to zero crossing
- Twist prompts stay visible while knob is touched
- Add snapToZeroCrossing() helper function
- Recording waveform: scrolling write-head visualization
- Recording stop: poll for sampler finalization before loading
- Shift+pad preview: 5% of selection, clamped 0.25s-5s
- Update help.json for new knob layout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Rebrand to scrape-cn: 3-tier strategy, Playwright optional

Major changes:
- Rename to scrape-cn, class ChinaScraper → ScrapeCN
- 3-tier strategy: HTTP fetch (zero deps) → Playwright → CDP Chrome
- Playwright is now a peer dependency (optional)
- New lib/http.js: fetchText, fetchJSON, parseLinks, parseJsonLD,
  parseInlineJSON — works without any browser
- Static methods ScrapeCN.fetch() and ScrapeCN.api() for zero-dep usage
- Rewrite README as a star-worthy tool page with feature matrix,
  strategy comparison, and concise API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Two-tier user memory: pinned (core) vs learned (ephemeral) facts

- Add 'pinned' column to user_facts with auto-migration for existing DBs
- Pinned facts are never evicted by LRU — only unpinned count toward cap
- Pin/unpin API endpoint: PATCH /v1/agent/memory/:id/pin
- System prompt labels 'Core facts' vs 'Learned' separately
- Settings UI: pin button with visual distinction (primary border/bg),
  counter shows 'N pinned · M learned'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 6 bugs found during full project code review

1. vehicules.js: Add null check after Store.findById() in _addMaintenance()
   - Would crash with "Cannot read property of null" if vehicle not found

2. etat-lieux.js: Use createSignalement() instead of private _post()
   - Also fix invalid urgence value 'faible' → 'normale' (not in Mongoose enum)
   - Fix type 'etat_lieux' → 'autre' (not in Signalement type enum)

3. driver-store.js: Add AbortController timeout to _delete() method
   - Was the only HTTP method missing timeout handling, could hang indefinitely

4. signalements.js: Add null check on btn.closest('form') in _selectType()
   - Prevents potential "Cannot read property of null" if form not found

5. messagerie.js: Add await to markConversationRead() async call
   - Ensures read status is properly updated before continuing

6. driver-app.js: Add null check on route in refresh button handler
   - Prevents crash if getCurrentRoute() returns undefined

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Migra de Streamlit para FastAPI + Tailwind CSS

BREAKING CHANGE: Remove Streamlit, usa FastAPI

- Backend FastAPI muito mais rapido
- Frontend HTML com Tailwind CSS (design moderno)
- Graficos interativos com Chart.js
- Drag & drop para upload de arquivos
- Tabela com busca em tempo real
- Health check em /health para UptimeRobot
- Consulta em background (nao trava a interface)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Engagement filter to recipients table in all 3 campaign detail pages

Filters recipient rows by per-email engagement state on the campaign
detail pages for Company Campaigns, Clinic Emails, and Outreach Emails.

Filter options: All / Opened / Clicked / Bounced / Failed+Suppressed / Not Opened

- Company Campaigns & Clinic Emails: client-side filter over already-loaded
  campaign_recipients array; dropdown in card header with filtered/total count
- Outreach Emails: server-side filter via new `engagement` param on
  GET /api/outreach-campaigns/[id]/recipients; re-fetches on filter change
- API: extend outreach recipients route with opened_at/clicked_at/status filters

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: reduce Sentry trace/profile sample rate from 100% to 10%

100% sample rate was flooding the self-hosted Sentry Relay with more
events than it could process, causing "failed to queue envelope" errors
and "deadline exceeded" on project state fetches. 10% still captures
enough data for meaningful error tracking and performance monitoring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: migrate Maria to OpenAIResponses and add output guardrails

- Switch Maria agent from OpenAIChat (/v1/chat/completions) to
  OpenAIResponses (/v1/responses) to fix GPT-5.4 error with
  reasoning_effort + function tools
- Add _is_error_response() output guardrail to detect and block
  internal error messages from reaching leads via WhatsApp
- Apply guardrail in both agent_manager (layer 1) and buffer_service
  (layer 2) for defense in depth
- Error handler now returns None instead of error string, and reports
  to Sentry instead of local file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Hero performance: remove scale animation from blurred orbs

Animating scale on elements with blur-[120px] forces the browser to
recalculate the blur filter every frame (very expensive). Changed to
opacity-only animation and reduced blur to 80px. Added will-change:opacity
so the browser can optimize without creating unnecessary compositing layers.

commit.message:
Track E: Prove resolveSingleFseTable RLE and repeat mode position specs (#740)

* feat: Prove resolveSingleFseTable RLE and repeat mode position specs

Add two theorems completing position tracking for 3 of 4 compression
modes in resolveSingleFseTable:
- resolveSingleFseTable_rle_pos: position advances by exactly 1
- resolveSingleFseTable_repeat_pos: position is unchanged

Combined with the existing predefined_pos theorem, only the FSE
compressed mode remains unspecified for position tracking.

Closes #736

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove ApplicationService namespace prefix

ApplicationService.js defines functions directly in the global scope
(function createApplicationRecord, function getApplicationForApplicant, etc)
rather than as methods on an object. Code.js was incorrectly calling them
as ApplicationService.functionName(), causing ReferenceError.

Changed all calls from ApplicationService.functionName() to functionName()
to match how Google Apps Script loads multiple .gs files into shared namespace.

This fixes the 'ApplicationService is not defined' error on membership application submission.

commit.message:
Add engine_runs persistence and engine agreement analysis

Persist execution metadata for every engine collection attempt (success
and failure) so we can answer "how often does X fail?" and track fetch
latency over time. New engine_runs table with status, timing, picks_count,
and error details.

Add convergence/agreement analysis that groups resolved engine picks by
how many engines agreed on each (run_date, ticker) pair, computing
hit rates, avg returns, MFE/MAE per convergence level plus pairwise
engine agreement stats. Available via --agreement-report CLI flag.

commit.message:
fix(ego-lint): expand CSS shell-class list from GNOME Shell SCSS source (#57)

## Summary

- Cross-referenced `KNOWN_SHELL_CLASSES` in `check-css.py` against GNOME
Shell's canonical SCSS widget files to add 13 missing shell classes
across 6 categories
- Added compound selector detection to `check_shell_class_override` to
avoid false positives on `.shell-class.my-ext-class` patterns
- Added test fixture and assertions for the new classes

### Classes added

| Category | Classes | SCSS Source |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| Panel | `panel-status-indicators-box`, `clock-display` | `_panel.scss`
|
| Popup menu | `popup-status-menu-item`, `panel-menu`, `background-menu`
| `_popovers.scss`, `popupMenu.js` |
| Overview | `window-picker`, `window-caption`, `window-close` |
`_window-picker.scss` |
| Dash / app grid | `dash-background`, `overview-icon`, `icon-grid`,
`app-folder` | `_dash.scss`, `_app-grid.scss` |
| Dialogs | `modal-dialog` | `_dialogs.scss` |
| Also from prior commit | `popup-menu-content`, `popup-menu-arrow`,
`popup-menu-boxpointer`, `popup-menu-icon`, `popup-menu-ornament`,
`popup-inactive-menu-item`, `popup-ornamented-menu-item`,
`quick-settings-grid`, `quick-menu-toggle`, `quick-toggle-menu`,
`quick-slider`, `notification-banner`, `calendar`, `events-button`,
`workspace-background`, `workspace-thumbnails`, `osd-window`, `slider` |
various |

### Note on issue scope

`.popup-menu-container` and `.popup-menu-box` (mentioned in #44) are
**not** standard GNOME Shell theme classes. They are custom classes the
media-controls extension adds to its own widgets
(`PanelButton.js:178,232`). Adding them to `KNOWN_SHELL_CLASSES` would
create false positives for extensions using these as their own class
names. The field test report was mistaken about their origin.

## Test plan

- [x] `bash tests/run-tests.sh` — all assertions pass
- [x] Fixture `css-shell-classes-extra@test` verifies new overrides
detected and compound selectors exempted
- [x] Added fixture coverage for panel (`panel-status-indicators-box`)
and window-picker (`window-caption`) categories

Closes #44

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use postinstall for prisma generate on Vercel

Vercel's Next.js builder runs next build directly, bypassing package.json
build scripts. Moving prisma generate to postinstall ensures the Prisma
client is regenerated with AI schema enums before the build starts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: créer route /dashboard (app/(dashboard)/dashboard/page.js)

L'ancien page.js dans le route group créait / pas /dashboard.
Suppression du fichier conflictuel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use deterministic URLs for VITE_API_URL like the working backup

The backup repo (integrity-home-healthcare) hardcoded VITE_API_URL as
https://<slug>-care-api.onrender.com in render.yaml — no API calls,
no race conditions. Our deploy service was creating the frontend with
empty envVars then trying to set VITE_API_URL after the build started.

Now: construct URLs deterministically from the service name (same
pattern as render.yaml.template), pass all env vars at service
creation time. No post-creation updates, no delays, no redeployments.
Also sets FRONTEND_URL on the backend at creation time.

commit.message:
feat(agents): add all five specialist agents

Research Agent: salary benchmarking + market demand trend analysis
Workforce Analytics Agent: attrition rate, risk level, tenure, segmentation
Financial Impact Agent: payroll cost modeling across salary scenarios
Strategy Agent: synthesises all agent outputs into strategic recommendation
Report Generator Agent: produces structured executive Markdown report

Each agent uses an isolated tool set and returns a typed Pydantic output.

Fix gallery: swap GSAP Reveal for IntersectionObserver in masonry grid

GSAP ScrollTrigger incorrectly calculates element positions inside CSS
columns layout, leaving column 2/3 items stuck at opacity:0. Replace with
native IntersectionObserver which is always layout-aware and fires reliably
for every item regardless of column position.
